name: Kingfish (SS-234)
events: USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army hospital ship Takachiko Maru (8154 GRT) in Formosa Strait in position 26?00'N, 122?18'E.
14 Jul 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lowrence, USN) arrived at New London, Connecticut.
5 Aug 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lowrence, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for the Panama Canal Zone.
17 Aug 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lowrence, USN) transited the Panama Canal.
31 Aug 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) arrived at Pearl Harbor from New London.
9 Sep 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in Japanese home waters.
1 Oct 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Yomei Maru (2860 GRT) off Ichiezaki, Japan in position 33°31'N, 135°26'E.
23 Oct 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese auxiliary gunboat Seikyo Maru (2608 GRT) at the entrance to Kii Suido, Honshu, Japan in position 33°20'N, 135°27'E.
3 Nov 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
25 Nov 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) departed from Midway for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Formosa.
7 Dec 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Hino Maru No.3 (4389 GRT) west of the Bonin Islands in position 23°59'N, 138°43'E.
28 Dec 1942 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Choyo Maru (5388 BRT) off the northwest coast of Formosa in position 24°46'N, 120°40'E.
7 Jan 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) sank two Japanese sailing vessels with gunfire west of the Bonin Islands in the following positions 25°46'N, 138°09'E and 25°31'N, 136°28'E.
23 Jan 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) ended her 2nd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
16 Feb 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 3rd war patrol. Once again she was ordered to patrol off Formosa.
4 Mar 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) sank a large Japanese sampan with gunfire off the Bonin Islands in position 24°23'N, 143°18'E.
17 Mar 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese transport ship Tenryugawa Maru (3883 GRT) in Formosa Strait in position 24°32'N, 120°15'E.
23 Mar 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) is damaged by depth charges north of Formosa in position 26°20'N, 121°55'E, and is forced to terminate her patrol.
9 Apr 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) ended her 3rd war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She is now ordered to the Mare Islands Navy Yard to be repaired and refitted. (1)
23 Jun 1943 With her repairs completed USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) arrived back at Pearl Harbor.
1 Jul 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.
25 Aug 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) ended her 4th war patrol at Fremantle, Australia.

21 Sep 1943 USS Kingfish (T/Cdr. V.L. Lawrence, USN) conducted exercises off Fremantle. The destroyer HrMs Tjerk Hiddes (Lt.Cdr. W.J. Kruys, RNethN) served as target ship during these exercises. Also A/S exercises were carried out. (2)
24 Sep 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) departed from Fremantle for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.
6 Oct 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) lands men and supplies on northeast coast of Borneo.
9 Oct 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese fleet oiler Hayatomo (14050 GRT) in the Sibitu Channel in position 05°09'N, 119°18'E.
10 Oct 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) lays mines off Cape Pepe, Makassar Strait, Celebes.
20 Oct 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Sana Maru (3365 GRT) off Banbon Bay, French Indochina in position 12°36'N, 109°30'E.
14 Nov 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. V.L. Lawrence) ended her 5th war patrol at Fremantle.
16 Dec 1943 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) departed from Fremantle for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.

3 Jan 1944 While attacking a Japanese convoy north-west of Miri, Borneo, USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) departed from torpedoed and sank the Japanese fleet tankers Bokuei Maru (5135 GRT) in position 06°58'N, 112°02'E, and the Ryuei Maru (5144 GRT) in position 08°06'N, 112°30'E.
7 Jan 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant tanker Fushimi Maru No.3 (4289 GRT) southwest of Palawan in position 09°27'N, 117°36'E.
26 Jan 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) ended her 6th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
19 Feb 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 7th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Mariana Islands area.
9 Apr 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) ended her 7th war patrol at Majuro.
1 May 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) departed from Majuro for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bonin Islands.
19 Jun 1944 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. H.L. Jukes) ended her 8th war patrol at Midway. She is now ordered to the Mare Islands Navy Yard for an overhaul.
1 Jul 1944 The Japanese merchant cargo ship Nikko Maru (3098 GRT) is sunk by a mine, most likely laid by USS Kingfish on 10 October 1943, off Makassar in position 05°38'S, 119°28'E.
12 Oct 1944 With her overhaul completed USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 9th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bonin Islands.
24 Oct 1944 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Ikutagawa Maru (2220 GRT) east of Chichi Jima in position 27°08'N, 143°13'E.

27 Oct 1944 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) torpedoed and sank the Japanese sank the Japanese landing ship T-138 (887 tons) and the Japanese army cargo vessel Tokai Maru No.4 (537 BRT) about 30 nautical miles north-north-east of Iwo Jima in position 25°22'N, 141°31'E.
28 Nov 1944 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) ended her 9th war patrol at Guam.
23 Dec 1944 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) departed from Guam for her 10th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bonin Islands.

3 Jan 1945 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Shoto Maru (572 GRT) and the Japanese merchant cargo ships Yaei Maru (1941 GRT) and Shibazono Maru (1831 BRT) about 200 nautical miles north of Chichi Jima in position 30°29'N, 142°03'E.
1 Feb 1945 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) ended her 10th war patrol at Guam.
6 Mar 1945 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) departed from Guam for her 11th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol south of Kyusyu.

6 Mar 1945 Around 1445 K/6, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Kingfish (T/Lt.Cdr. T.E. Harper, USN) departed Guam to proceed to their patrol area in the East China Sea. For USS Kingfish, which had refitted at Guam, this was her 11th war patrol.
These submarines were to operate as a coordinated attack group which was also to be joined later by USS Sawfish (T/Lt.Cdr. D.H. Pugh, USN).
They were escorted out until around 1845 K/6 by USS Fanning (T/Cdr. J.C. Bentley, USN). (3)

15 Mar 1945 In the evening USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Kingfish (T/Lt.Cdr. T.E. Harper, USN) were joined USS Sawfish (T/Lt.Cdr. D.H. Pugh, USN). The coordinated attack group is now complete. (4)

19 Mar 1945 In the evening USS Sawfish (T/Lt.Cdr. D.H. Pugh, USN) left the coordinated attack group which was further made up of USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Kingfish (T/Lt.Cdr. T.E. Harper, USN). USS Sawfish had been assigned other duties temporary. She rejoined the group on March 25th. (4)

9 Apr 1945 The coordinated attack group made up of USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN), USS Kingfish (T/Lt.Cdr. T.E. Harper, USN) and USS Sawfish (T/Lt.Cdr. D.H. Pugh, USN) was disbanded when USS Kingfish and USS Sawfish left the area. (4)
25 Apr 1945 USS Kingfish (Cdr. T.E. Harper) ended her 11th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
17 Jun 1945 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Reegan) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 12th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in Japanese home waters and the Kuril Islands.

2 Jul 1945 Around 1645 K/2, USS Lionfish (T/Lt.Cdr. B.M. Ganyard, USN) and USS Kingfish (T/Lt.Cdr. T.D. Keegan, USN) departed Guam for their patrol areas. They were escorted until 2030 K/2 by USS PC-1587 (Lt. D.N. Perrine, USNR).
The submarines parted company with each other around 2200 I/4.
5 Aug 1945 USS Kingfish (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Reegan) sank two Japanese sampans with gunfire off Paramushir, Kuril Islands in position 50°43'N, 156°38'E.
